pieces.text: the us navy is now blockading the strait of hormuz. a quarter of the world's seaborne oil passes through it.
oil is close to $100 a barrel and still climbing. goldman sachs says if it stays there, the us and europe tip into recession.
europe entered this crisis with gas storage at just 30% capacity. dutch gas prices have nearly doubled since march.
the ecb held rates steady but cut its eurozone growth forecast to 0.9% this year. it used the word 'stagflation' for the first time in years.
the us added 178,000 jobs in march, which sounds fine. but february was minus 133,000, so nobody really knows what's happening.
the imf releases its full global economic outlook tomorrow. the draft already says wars cause bigger economic damage than financial crises do.
